examples.title: Typedef with Structures
examples.description: Demonstrates how typedef simplifies structure declarations and usage
examples.explanation: Shows two equivalent ways to declare structures. The typedef version is more concise as it eliminates the need to write 'struct' keyword when declaring variables.

examples.title: Typedef with Pointers
examples.description: Shows how typedef creates cleaner pointer type aliases
examples.explanation: Demonstrates various pointer typedefs. IntPtr simplifies int* declarations. ConstIntPtr creates a constant pointer, and IntConstPtr creates a pointer to constant data.

examples.title: Typedef for Function Pointers
examples.description: Shows how typedef simplifies complex function pointer declarations
examples.explanation: Function pointer syntax is complex and hard to read. typedef creates meaningful names (CompareFunc, Callback) that make code much more readable and maintainable.

examples.title: Typedef for Linked List Node
examples.description: Demonstrates typedef with self-referential structures for linked lists
examples.explanation: Shows typedef with self-referential structures for linked lists. typedef allows declaring Node* pointers easily. Note that inside the struct, we must use 'struct Node*' not just 'Node*' because the typedef isn't complete yet.
